input.headline: I made mistakes on TV, he made his on a field'

raw.headline: I made mistakes on TV, he made his on a field'

winner: The weather forecaster said, "Tomorrow will be sunny with a chance of me repeating yesterday's forecast." The quarterback replied, "That's nothing, on the field I have a 50% chance of repeating last week's pass!

candidates.text: A man said, "I messed up on live TV." The athlete replied, "Well, I made my fumbles on the field!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is clear and plays on the word 'fumbles,' which works well in both contexts. It's concise and has a nice twist, though the setup could be slightly tighter for maximum impact."}

candidates.text: The interviewer said, "You made mistakes on TV," and the athlete replied, "Well, at least I didn't make mine in front of millions watching a game!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise and has a mild twist with the comparison between TV and a field, but the humor is somewhat generic and lacks strong specificity or surprise."}

candidates.text: The weather forecaster said, "Tomorrow will be sunny with a chance of me repeating yesterday's forecast." The quarterback replied, "That's nothing, on the field I have a 50% chance of repeating last week's pass!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific, and uses a clever twist with the weather forecaster and quarterback comparing their chances of repeating mistakes."}

candidates.text: The weatherman said it would be sunny and clear, but it turned out to be cloudy with a chance of footballs.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat surprising and specific, but the connection to the headline is weak and the humor feels forced."}

candidates.text: I guess we both learned that live performances can be a bit risky... one of us just happened to be playing in front of millions.
